ad_hominem.text: Does it keep the argument aimed at the idea itself?
Does it judge the claim on its own merits, whoever said it?
Does it give the original point a direct answer?

strawman.text: Does it take on the claim exactly as the other person made it?
Does it restate the other side in a way they'd accept?

tu_quoque.text: Does it answer the criticism on its content, whoever raised it?
If it raises an inconsistency, is that inconsistency truly the topic at hand?

appeal_to_authority.text: Is it built on reasons you could check for yourself?
If it cites an expert, does the expert's specialty match this exact topic?
Would the case still hold up with the famous name left out?

appeal_to_emotion.text: Is there still a real reason once fear, pity, or anger is set aside?
Would its reasons still stand if the case were put calmly?
Is the thing pushing you toward the conclusion an actual reason?

appeal_to_nature.text: Does it weigh good and bad by real effects?
Does it look at this specific case for real benefit or harm?
Does the verdict rest on evidence of benefit or harm, whatever is natural?

bandwagon.text: Does the case rest on reasons, however many people agree?
Does it give evidence beyond "most people" or "everyone" agreeing?

hasty_generalization.text: Does how broad the conclusion is match how much evidence backs it?
Are the examples a fair, varied sample of the whole?

false_cause.text: Does it show how one thing actually causes the other, beyond timing?
Does it rule out other explanations like coincidence or a shared cause?
Is the case more than "one came first, so it caused the other"?

slippery_slope.text: Are the steps from the first move to the feared outcome backed up?
Is the predicted outcome the right size for the small step discussed?
Is each link in the chain shown to be likely on its own?

false_dilemma.text: Does it lay out the full range of real options?
Does it leave a workable middle ground or "do both" on the table?
Does it allow positions beyond just two camps?

circular_reasoning.text: Is the support a genuinely separate reason from the conclusion?
Would it persuade someone still on the fence about the conclusion?
Does the reason add new information beyond the conclusion itself?

argument_from_incredulity.text: Does it reject the claim on actual evidence against it?
Does it back its doubt with a checkable reason?
Does the doubt point to something specific and unlikely in the claim itself?

poisoning_the_well.text: Does it let the point get made first, then answer it?
Does it judge each thing the person says on its own?

genetic_fallacy.text: Does it judge the idea by what it says today, on its own terms?
Does it look at the idea's worth now, no matter how it started?

red_herring.text: Does it stay on the topic being discussed?
Does the point it raises bear on the original question?

whataboutism.text: Does it answer the criticism on its own terms?
Does it deal with the original charge directly?

moving_the_goalposts.text: Does it accept the asked-for evidence as settling the point?
Does it stick to the standard it set up front?

non_sequitur.text: Does the conclusion follow from the reasons given?
Does it spell out the step linking reasons to conclusion?

appeal_to_fear.text: Does it show the feared outcome is actually likely?
Does it size the danger to the known facts?

appeal_to_pity.text: Does it give reasons the point holds on its merits?
Does any hardship it raises really bear on what's being decided?

appeal_to_ridicule.text: Does it give an actual reason the view is wrong?
Does it engage the view as its holder actually meant it?

appeal_to_spite.text: Does the case work for a reader with no grudge involved?
Does any wrongdoing it raises really bear on what's being decided?

appeal_to_tradition.text: Does it give reasons the practice works today?
Does it show what the long practice produced, beyond just lasting?

appeal_to_novelty.text: Does it give reasons the new thing actually works better?
Does it show a real gain over the old option, beyond being latest?

snob_appeal.text: Does it point to the thing's own qualities?
Would the case still work if everyone could have it?

false_equivalence.text: Does it compare things that really match in scale and seriousness?
Does it limit the comparison to what the two things actually share?

perfect_solution.text: Does it weigh the leftover flaws against the real gains?
Does it judge the option against the real alternatives on the table?

middle_ground.text: Does it back the middle position with reasons of its own?
Does it let the better-backed side win, even if that side is an extreme?

begging_the_question.text: Does it back up its key starting premise with support of its own?
Does it argue the disputed point out in the open?

loaded_question.text: Does the question leave the disputed point genuinely open?
Can you give a straight answer and keep your actual position?

appeal_to_ignorance.text: Does it give positive evidence for the claim itself?
Does it treat an unproven claim as still an open question?

shifting_burden_of_proof.text: Does the person making the claim bring their own support for it?
Is the claim treated as needing support before it counts?

argument_from_silence.text: Does it build on what the source actually says?
Does it show the source would surely mention this before reading meaning into its silence?

anecdotal.text: Does it back the claim with evidence beyond one person's story?
Does the wider data do the convincing here?

cherry_picking.text: Does it weigh the evidence that points the other way too?
Does it show the full mix of cases, favorable and unfavorable?

sampling_bias.text: Does its sample fairly stand for the whole group?
Does the sample match the group the conclusion is about?

overgeneralization.text: Does it keep the rule scoped to where it actually holds?
Does it leave room for exceptions where the case is different?

post_hoc.text: Does it give evidence beyond one thing coming after another?
Does it show the pattern repeating beyond a single before-and-after?

single_cause.text: Does it allow that several causes fed the outcome?
Does it weigh the other contributing causes too?

correlation_causation.text: Does it show one thing actually causes the other, beyond them tracking together?
Does it show which way the cause actually runs?

weak_analogy.text: Do the shared traits it points to matter for the conclusion?
Do the similarities run deep enough to actually carry the conclusion?

false_analogy.text: Do the two cases match where the point depends on it?
Does the parallel hold right where the argument turns on it?

apples_to_oranges.text: Does it measure both by one shared standard that fits each of them?
Are the two alike enough in kind that comparing them means something?

equivocation.text: Does each key word keep one meaning from start to finish?
Does it still work when the key term is pinned to one fixed meaning throughout?

amphiboly.text: Does the key sentence have just one clear way to read it?
Is the key sentence worded so only one meaning is possible?

accent.text: Does it read the statement with its plain, even emphasis?
Does it still mean the same with the original wording and stress?

definist.text: Does its definition of the key word leave the conclusion open?
Does the definition it uses match the word's normal meaning?

composition.text: Does it give separate proof that the whole itself has the trait?
Does it show the trait still holds when the parts combine?

division.text: Does it give separate proof that each part has the trait?
Does it show how the trait reaches each individual part?

ecological_fallacy.text: Does it keep a group average as a claim about the group?
Does it back a claim about one person with evidence about that person?

affirming_the_consequent.text: Does it consider other ways the result could have come about?
Does it check and rule out other routes to the same result first?

denying_the_antecedent.text: Does it allow the result could still happen another way, even with the condition absent?
Does it show the stated condition is the only path before ruling the result out?

base_rate_neglect.text: Does it weigh how common the thing is overall, alongside the test result?
Does it fold in how rare the thing is when judging the odds?

gamblers_fallacy.text: Does it keep each independent try at the same odds?
Does it treat each new try as a fresh, independent chance?

regression_fallacy.text: Does it notice an extreme case tends to swing back toward normal?
Does it rule out a natural swing back to average first?

survivorship_bias.text: Does it count the cases that dropped out along the way too?
Does it check whether the dropped-out cases shared that same trait?

texas_sharpshooter.text: Does it name the pattern in advance, before looking at the results?
Does it weigh all the cases, the scattered ones along with the cluster?

appeal_to_consequences.text: Does it tie the verdict to evidence about what is actually true?
When it rejects a claim, is that grounded in evidence the claim is false?

appeal_to_motive.text: Does it weigh the claim on the evidence, whatever the speaker stands to gain?
If it flags a motive, does it still judge the point on its own evidence?

two_wrongs.text: Does it defend the act on its own terms?
Does it judge the act on its own, treating "others did it too" as no excuse?

ad_hominem_circumstantial.text: Does it address the view itself, whatever the speaker's situation?
Does the view still get judged on its merits after the speaker's situation is noted?

complex_question.text: Does it ask about one thing at a time?
Does it let each part of the question be answered on its own?

no_true_scotsman.text: Does it let a genuine counterexample count against the claim?
Does it stick to the membership rules it set up front?

suppressed_correlative.text: Does it keep both sides of the pair genuinely meaningful?
Does it keep a real line between the two options?

appeal_to_force.text: Does it rest on reasons you are free to weigh and turn down?
Does it leave you free to disagree, with no penalty for doing so?

appeal_to_flattery.text: Does it persuade with reasons a stranger could weigh?
Does it still hold once any praise is set aside, because real reasons back it?

appeal_to_wealth.text: Does it show quality by how the thing actually performs?
Does it weigh the claim on its reasons, rich speaker or poor?

appeal_to_common_sense.text: Does it give actual reasons beyond calling the claim obvious?
Does it answer doubts with facts, treating the doubter as reasonable?

loaded_language.text: Does it back the judgment with reasons beyond charged wording?
Does it still hold up when charged words are swapped for plain neutral ones?

weasel_words.text: Does it name a specific source you could look up?
Does it put real evidence behind the hedged claim?

continuum_fallacy.text: Does it accept the real gap between clear cases, even with no exact cutoff?
Does it accept that many small steps can add up to a real difference?

reification.text: Does it treat "society" or "the market" as shorthand for real people acting?
Does it rest on the real people and facts behind the abstraction?

buckets.name: It goes after the person, not the point
buckets.prompt: It answers who is talking, or changes the subject, instead of the actual claim. Like: “you would say that, you failed a class.”

buckets.name: It tugs on feelings, status, or the crowd
buckets.prompt: Instead of giving reasons, it leans on emotion, on who said it, or on how many agree. Like: “everyone knows this is true.”

buckets.name: It sneaks in something unproven
buckets.prompt: It treats a shaky starting point as already settled, or just restates the claim as its own proof. Like: “why are you against helping people?”

buckets.name: It reaches further than the evidence
buckets.prompt: It jumps to a big conclusion from thin evidence, a bad comparison, or a wrong cause. Like: “my uncle smoked and lived to 90, so it is fine.”

buckets.name: It hides behind slippery wording
buckets.prompt: A key word quietly shifts meaning, or vague and loaded wording does the real work. Like calling the same act “brave” or “reckless” to win the point.

buckets.name: The steps or numbers do not connect
buckets.prompt: The if-then reasoning takes a wrong turn, or the numbers and chances are read wrong. Like: “if it rained the ground is wet; the ground is wet, so it rained.”
